title: Puri Bhaji
description: Puri Bhaji is a classic Indian breakfast featuring crispy, deep-fried whole wheat puris served with a lightly spiced potato curry. Simple, comforting, and satisfying, it is a favorite across many regions of India.

ingredients.title: For the Aloo Bhaji (Potato Curry)
items: 1 tsp amchur (dry mango powder) or a squeeze of lemon juice

instruction.title: Step 1: Dough Preparation
steps: In a large bowl, mix wheat flour, semolina, and salt.
Add oil and rub it into the flour until crumbly.
Gradually add water and knead into a smooth, firm dough (like chapati dough but slightly stiffer).

instruction.title: Step 2: Rolling
steps: Divide dough into small lemon-sized balls.
Roll each ball into a smooth, slightly thick disc (not too thin; ~3–4 mm).

instruction.title: Step 3: Frying the Puri
steps: Heat oil in a deep frying pan (medium-high).
Test with a small piece of dough; it should rise immediately.
Carefully slide each puri into the oil and fry until golden and puffed.

instruction.title: Step 4: Making the Aloo Bhaji
steps: Boil potatoes until soft but firm. Drain.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin and mustard seeds; let them splutter.
Add green chili and ginger paste; sauté for 30 seconds.
Mash slightly with the back of a spoon, keeping some chunks for texture.
Season with salt and amchur/lemon juice.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Serve the puris hot with freshly prepared aloo bhaji.
Pair with pickle, yogurt, or masala chai for a complete meal.

chefTips: Crispier puris: Add semolina and ensure the dough is slightly stiff. Resting dough helps puff.
Perfect bhaji: Slightly mash potatoes rather than making a puree. Add a hint of tang (amchur or lemon) for freshness.
Serving: Serve immediately; puris lose crispness if left too long.
